title: Operating Leverage and Business Scalability
author: Jane Economist
url: https://example.com/operating-leverage
date_saved: 2026-06-16T00:00:00Z
content_hash: placeholder
tags: ["economics", "business", "scalability"]
---

Operating leverage describes the degree to which a business can increase operating income by increasing revenue. Companies with high fixed costs and low variable costs exhibit high operating leverage — once fixed costs are covered, each additional unit of revenue flows more directly to profit.

A software company exemplifies high operating leverage: the cost to serve an additional customer is near zero once the product is built. This stands in contrast to a labor-intensive services business, where each new client requires proportional additional headcount and direct costs.

Understanding operating leverage is critical for investors analyzing growth scenarios. A business with high operating leverage can generate outsized returns during expansionary periods, but it also faces magnified losses during contractions. The key leverage ratio is the contribution margin divided by operating income.
